schema.appDescription: Calculate KVP maturity value and doubling period. Current KVP interest rate is 7.5% p.a. with 115-month doubling period. Issued at post offices.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the KVP Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option.

faq.a: KVP is a Post Office saving certificate that doubles your money in approximately 115 months (9 years 7 months) at the current rate of 7.5% p.a. Available at all post offices and authorised banks. There is no maximum investment limit. Interest is compounded annually.

KVP interest is fully taxable as 'income from other sources' at your applicable income tax slab rate. No TDS is deducted but you must declare the interest annually in your ITR. KVP does not offer any Section 80C deduction.

faq.q: Can KVP be transferred and pledged?
faq.a: Yes. KVP certificates can be transferred between individuals (on death or as a gift) and can be pledged as security for loans. They can be encashed prematurely after 2.5 years at reduced rates.
Yes, the KVP Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the KVP Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh KVP at 7.5% for 115 months
examples.text: ₹1 lakh invested in KVP at 7.5% p.a. doubles to ₹2 lakh in 115 months (9 years 7 months). Annual compounding gives exact maturity: ₹2,00,000. Effective CAGR confirmation: 7.5%.

examples.heading: ₹5 lakh KVP — interest income over tenure
examples.text: ₹5 lakh KVP at 7.5% matures at ₹10 lakh after 115 months, earning ₹5 lakh interest. This interest is taxable at slab rates — at 20% slab, tax on ₹5 lakh is ₹1,04,000 (with cess).
